info.name: WhatNext Voice (Socket.IO over WebSocket)
info.description:
Test the WhatNext voice microservice from Postman.

The backend uses **Socket.IO v4** (Engine.IO v4). Postman's dedicated Socket.IO request type cannot send the `auth` object the server needs, so this collection uses a **raw WebSocket request** and speaks the Socket.IO framing by hand. Authentication is sent in the Socket.IO CONNECT frame: `40{"token":"<JWT>"}` — exactly what the gateway reads from `socket.handshake.auth.token`.

IMPORTANT: Postman does not fully import/export WebSocket requests. If the request below does not open as a WebSocket, create one manually: New > WebSocket Request, paste the URL `{{ws_url}}`, Connect, then send the frames listed in the request description (and in the collection README).

Variables:
- `ws_url`  -> ws://localhost:8980/socket.io/?EIO=4&transport=websocket
- `jwt`     -> a valid JWT access token (any decodable token with a `sub` claim works in development)

See docs/postman/README.md for full step-by-step instructions and expected responses.

request.description:
Raw WebSocket to the Socket.IO endpoint.

After you click Connect, the server (Engine.IO) sends an OPEN frame starting with `0{...}`. Then send these text frames IN ORDER from the Postman message composer (each line is one message):

1) Connect the Socket.IO namespace WITH auth (replaces handshake.auth):
   40{"token":"{{jwt}}"}
   -> expect: 40{"sid":"..."}        (success)
   -> on auth failure: 44{"message":"Missing auth token"}

2) Start a voice session:
   42["voice:start"]
   -> expect: 42["voice:ready",{"userId":"...","encoding":"linear16","sampleRate":16000}]
   -> if Deepgram key missing: 42["voice:warning",{"message":"Deepgram API key is not configured"}]

3) Liveness check:
   42["voice:ping"]
   -> expect: 42["voice:pong",{"timestamp":"..."}]

4) Manually finalize the current utterance:
   42["voice:commit"]
   -> expect: 42["voice:committed",{"timestamp":"..."}]

5) End the session:
   42["voice:stop"]
   -> expect: 42["voice:closed"]

KEEP-ALIVE: the server sends Engine.IO ping `2` periodically; reply with `3` (pong) to avoid a timeout disconnect (~20s).

LIMITATION: `voice:audio` carries raw binary PCM (Socket.IO BINARY_EVENT, packet `45` + separate binary frames). This is impractical to send by hand in Postman, so real transcription cannot be exercised here. Use the web client (whatnext_front) or `npm run voice:file` for end-to-end audio tests.
